title: AI Data Center Optical Interconnect Buildout
catalyst: NVIDIA's next-generation GPU platforms (Blackwell Ultra, Rubin) require dramatically more bandwidth between chips, driving massive investment into optical interconnect technology. NVIDIA has publicly committed to co-packaged optics and active electrical cables, creating a multi-billion dollar TAM for suppliers of high-speed connectivity components.
thesis: As AI clusters scale from thousands to millions of GPUs, the interconnect bottleneck becomes the binding constraint on performance. Electrical cables can't keep up beyond 800G — optical solutions are the only path to 1.6T and 3.2T speeds needed for next-gen training runs. Companies designing the silicon and optics for these links are in the critical path of every hyperscaler's capex plan.

ticker_rationale.CRDO: Credo Technology is the leading supplier of Active Electrical Cables (AECs) for AI data centers, with 201% YoY revenue growth driven by hyperscaler demand. Their SerDes IP and AEC technology sit directly in NVIDIA's supply chain for rack-scale AI interconnects.
ticker_rationale.MRVL: Marvell Technology's custom silicon business (doubling to $1.5B in FY2026) designs the connectivity chips that go inside AI networking equipment. Their PAM4 DSPs and custom ASIC platform serve every major hyperscaler building AI infrastructure.

risks: NVIDIA could vertically integrate interconnect technology, reducing reliance on third-party suppliers
AI capex spending could decelerate if training efficiency gains reduce hardware demand
Competitive pressure from Broadcom's custom silicon business could compress margins
sources: NVIDIA GTC 2026 — co-packaged optics and Rubin platform roadmap
Credo Technology Q3 FY2026 Earnings — 201% YoY revenue growth
Marvell Technology FY2026 Investor Day — custom silicon doubling
